$comment: PROTO-EARTH at the giant impact (~4.51 Ga) - a DIFFERENT BODY from modern Earth, which is the RESULT: proto-Earth + Theia - the Moon - what escaped. Pointing the birth scene at earth.json gave a post-impact planet of 1.115 Earth masses, 11% too heavy, because it added a Mars-sized body to a planet that already contained it. Mass here is 0.910 M_earth: what the budget demands, and independently what the giant-impact literature gives (~0.9). NO CONTINENTS, and no surface map, because there were none. At 4.51 Ga Earth was a MAGMA OCEAN: the oldest continental material we have is ~4.4 Ga zircon grains, the oldest surviving crust ~4.0 Ga, and paleogeographic reconstructions do not meaningfully reach past ~1 Ga. There is nothing to reconstruct, so nothing is drawn. The surface is molten peridotite rather than a basalt crust, and the declared geotherm (1,900 K at the surface) sits above peridotite's 1,393 K melt point - so MOLTEN emerges from the phase model and the glow emerges from the incandescence law. Neither is painted on. Layer radii are modern Earth's scaled by (0.910)^(1/3) at unchanged densities - FLAGGED: a less massive, hotter, fully molten planet is less compressed than this, so the real density profile differs somewhat. ATMOSPHERE: 4.99e20 kg, ~97x modern Earth's air, which the engine weighs into ~100 bar at this surface gravity. NOT modern air - a magma ocean degasses a massive steam/CO2 envelope, and the literature puts Hadean steam atmospheres over a magma ocean at the ~100 bar scale (Abe & Matsui 1985/1988; Zahnle et al.). For calibration the atmosphere AFTER the magma ocean had solidified is independently put at >27 bar with a ~503 K surface - and that is the cooled, settled end state, so the ocean-phase envelope is heavier. Zero was the previous declaration and it was wrong in an interesting way: it treated 'not modern air' as 'no air', when in fact this planet has FAR more atmosphere than today's, just made of different things and far hotter. COMPOSITION, from Zahnle et al., 'Earth's Earliest Atmospheres': the impact-degassed atmosphere is STEAM-DOMINATED ('the major gas is usually water vapor') and reduced, and once the mantle solidified the steam condensed to a ~500 K ocean under ~100 bars of CO2. So water first, CO2 second, then the high-temperature sulfur species (SO2, not the sulfuric acid of Venus's cloud deck - that needs cool altitudes to condense) and reduced traces. FLAGGED: the fractions are representative, not a solved chemistry; the same paper notes CO/CH4 crosses over near 1000-1200 K depending on pressure, so the reduced end is temperature-dependent rather than fixed. FLAGGED HARDER: rayleigh_tau still takes only a pressure ratio and is hardcoded to AIR, so this declared composition does not yet change how the atmosphere scatters. It changes the scale height (and so the twilight) via molar mass, but not the colour. Making tau a property of the declared gas is what the Mars/CO2 scene needs too.
name: Proto-Earth
